title: The recycling question
desc: Learn the rubbish rules from your neighbour
setting: Mrs. Bucher finds you puzzling over the bins in the building's courtyard.

steps.speaker: Frau Bucher
steps.es: Bruuched Sie Hilf mit em Abfall?
steps.en: Do you need help with the rubbish?

options.es: Ja, bitte! Ich verstah d'Regle nöd ganz.
options.en: Yes, please! I don't quite understand the rules.
options.feedback: Honest and friendly — asking a neighbour is exactly how you learn this.
Pride won't help here — the rules are genuinely tricky. Accept the offer.
It really does matter here — wrong sorting can mean a fine. Take the help.

steps.speaker: Frau Bucher
steps.es: Also: s'Glaas da ane, s'Papier separat.
steps.en: So: the glass over there, the paper separately.

options.en: I see, and the normal trash?
options.feedback: Great follow-up — you're getting the system. 'Güsel' is the local word for trash.
Repeating it back locks it in. Good.
It is at first! But she's explaining — stay with it.

steps.speaker: Frau Bucher
steps.es: De Güsel bruucht en offizielle Sack, mit Gebühr.
steps.en: The trash needs an official bag, with a fee.

options.es: Aha, drum sind die Säck spezial. Guet z'wüsse!
options.en: Ah, that's why those bags are special. Good to know!
options.feedback: You connected the dots — exactly the insight that saves you a fine.

options.es: Wo chan ich die Säck choufe?
options.en: Where can I buy those bags?
options.feedback: Practical and smart — that's the next thing you need to know.
It may seem odd, but it's the system — better to just learn it than judge it.

steps.speaker: Frau Bucher
steps.es: Sie lärned schnäll! Bald sind Sie e richtige Lozärner.
steps.en: You learn fast! Soon you'll be a real Lucerner.

options.es: Dank Ihne, Frau Bucher! Merci viilmal.
options.en: Thanks to you, Mrs. Bucher! Thank you very much.
options.feedback: Gracious — crediting her warmth deepens the bond. Lovely.

options.es: Das hoff ich! Merci für d'Hilf.
options.en: I hope so! Thanks for the help.
options.feedback: Warm and genuine. You're building a real neighbourly tie.
A warmer reply would honor her kind words better.
